meaning: vt.偷偷插入,使混入,硬卖给,私自添加,把...强加(于),把...塞(给)
examples: You can't foist your ideas on me.
Should not foist their own opinions into the original book.
And so we see the first in the queue to foist coal horrors upon us already knocking at the door.
The problem is that a 4tn euros EFSF would foist an enormous potential liability on Germany.
So the government may end up using new planning powers to foist development on some unwilling communities.
